fork-dip chopsticks into dressing to save tons of calories
order rolls 'naruto' = wrapped in cucumber instead of rice
whitefish/seabass/crab = lightest; eel/mackerel/salmon = heaviest
no more than 2 rolls per meal

avoid_items: deep-fried items (crispy duck, fried tofu, crispy rolls)
opt_for_items: summer rolls/fresh spring rolls (140 cal each)
calorie_data: summer rolls: 140 cal each
Pad Thai: 350 cal per cup
unique_hacks: Spring = deep-fried. Summer = not deep-fried. Choose accordingly.
fried tofu acts like a soybean sponge - sucks up oil. Ask for sauteed instead.
Thai tea = condensed milk + fistfuls of sugar = blood sugar spike = fat storage
skip dipping sauce on summer rolls (extra sodium and sugar)
Chicken Larb = high protein Thai 'salad' in lettuce wraps
cuisines.new_value_for_db: HIGH - the spring vs summer roll distinction is memorable coaching content. Comprehensive Thai ordering guide.

Cucumber slices instead of pita for dipping

Pho: order Nho (smallest) size
On days you eat Banh Mi, eat high protein + veggies for other meals to compensate

db_stub_restaurants.note: All 50 restaurants in DB have 17-65 ingredients. These are chains with spider-scraped data. The Restaurant Bible covers CUISINES (Afghan, Thai, Korean BBQ, etc.) which are NOT represented as restaurants in the DB at all.
